commit.message:
feat: support go install and fix devcontainer (#39)

* feat: support go install by moving CLI entrypoint to repo root

Move package main from cmd/ to the repository root so that
`go install github.com/manisbindra/sbi@latest` produces a binary
named `sbi` instead of the unusable `cmd`.

- Move all cmd/*.go files to repo root, remove cmd/ directory
- Rename CLI identity from daily-recommendations to sbi
- Update Taskfile.yml: project name and build path
- Update .goreleaser.yml: project name, IDs, remove dir: cmd
- Update CI workflows: binary paths in nightly-scan and scan-smoke-test
- Update README.md: add go install section, update CLI examples
- Update AGENTS.md: binary name, examples, project structure

Closes #38

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add sudo to devcontainer install-tools.sh for /usr/local/bin writes

onCreateCommand runs as the vscode user which lacks write permissions
to /usr/local/bin. Add sudo to the syft, trivy, and golangci-lint
install commands.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: update devcontainer Go image from 1.24 to 1.26

go.mod requires go >= 1.26.1 but the devcontainer image was pinned to
Go 1.24, causing build and test failures inside the container.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: update go.mod to Go 1.26.2 to resolve stdlib vulnerabilities

Go 1.26.1 has known vulnerabilities in crypto/x509, crypto/tls,
archive/tar, and html/template (GO-2026-4864 through GO-2026-4947),
all fixed in Go 1.26.2. govulncheck now passes cleanly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review comments

- Update reset-database.yml binary path from daily-recommendations to sbi
- Fix goreleaser archive: repositories.txt → repositories.json
- README: restructure Quick Start to use go install, add Build from Source
- AGENTS.md: add go install command to CLI Usage section

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Route switching: keeps currentEp, falls back to EP1 if not in new route
- Episode list rendered from currentRoute.episodes (CMS data)

commit.message:
anime: fix manifestLoadError (HLS auth) + SSE progressive source loading

Root cause of manifestLoadError: HLS.js fetched /api/tv/stream without the
Authorization header → got 401 JSON → couldn't parse as m3u8 manifest.
Fix: add xhrSetup to Hls config so every segment/manifest request includes
Bearer token.

Progressive source loading via SSE:
- /api/anime/find-stream now streams text/event-stream responses
- Each site that responds sends data:{type:'source',...} immediately
- Frontend reads stream progressively: spinner hides on first source,
  more sources appear in the source bar as they arrive
- Hard cap 15s (was 10s), per-site timeout stays 5s
- Deduplication by URL on the server side

Fix dynamic Function new.target leak and generator callable dispatch

- Add push_dynamic_function_call_new_target() helper to isolate
  new.target for BUILTIN_CTOR_FUNCTION/BUILTIN_NEW_FUNCTION calls,
  preventing outer constructor new.target from leaking into
  CreateDynamicFunction and corrupting the prototype chain.

- Fix __fn_body__ callable_expr to use function*/async function/
  async function* for generator/async dynamic functions.

- Split eval+call for generators: eval the function expression first,
  then call it separately so the generator's bytecode outlives the
  eval scope (fixes generators returning {done:true} immediately).

- Route all dynamic generator calls in runner.rs through
  vm_call_function_value instead of manual yield parsing, fixing
  return value handling in dynamic generators.

commit.message:
fix(hostexec): resolve relative argv0 against cwd for rule matching

When an agent runs `cd backend && ./gradlew spotlessApply`, the Python
wrapper sends argv0="./gradlew" with cwd="/workspace/backend". The rule
defines argv0="./backend/gradlew" (workspace-root-relative). matchRule
compared these directly and failed to match, causing a fallback error:
"relative argv0 fallback is not supported".

Fix matchRule to accept an optional MatchContext (cwd + workspaceRoot).
When direct relative-path comparison fails, resolve the actual argv0
against cwd to an absolute path, then re-derive the workspace-relative
path for comparison. This is done in the broker (TS) where both cwd and
workspaceRoot are already available, avoiding changes to the Python
wrapper or new environment variables.

commit.message:
feat: add eslint-plugin-react-you-might-not-need-an-effect (#988)

Adds
[`eslint-plugin-react-you-might-not-need-an-effect`](https://github.com/nickjvandyke/eslint-plugin-react-you-might-not-need-an-effect)
to catch unnecessary `useEffect` usage — derived state, chained state
updates, event handlers in effects, and similar anti-patterns.

## Changes

- **`package.json`**: adds
`eslint-plugin-react-you-might-not-need-an-effect` as a dev dependency
- **`eslint.config.js`**: imports and spreads the plugin's `recommended`
flat config

```js
import reactYouMightNotNeedAnEffect from "eslint-plugin-react-you-might-not-need-an-effect"

export default [
  // ...
  reactYouMightNotNeedAnEffect.configs.recommended,
  // ...
]
```

Enables 9 rules as warnings covering: `no-derived-state`,
`no-chain-state-updates`, `no-event-handler`, `no-initialize-state`,
`no-pass-data-to-parent`, `no-pass-live-state-to-parent`,
`no-adjust-state-on-prop-change`, `no-reset-all-state-on-prop-change`,
`no-empty-effect`.

commit.message:
fix(e2e): avoid Keycloak redirect DNS in stack smoke health checks

Playwright followed 302 to http://keycloak:8080; that hostname is not resolvable on the CI host. Use maxRedirects: 0 and treat 302 to OAuth like the curl-based wait loop.
